nodes.label: Dense vs Sparse Storage
nodes.rationale: Dense is preferred for hot paths (ODE integration) due to stack allocation and no heap; Sparse is preferred for high N/M with structural zeros to save memory. Trade-off: dense is constexpr-safe and kernel-optimized; sparse has O(log nnz) access and O(nnz_a+nnz_b) add.

nodes.label: Library Architecture
nodes.rationale: Deliberately avoids lazy expression templates because the fixed-shape std::array payload and eager kernel writes are already allocation-free under RVO. Expression templates would add compile complexity without measured runtime win.

nodes.label: Dense Storage Policy
nodes.rationale: Stack-allocated std::array; no heap; constexpr-safe; all kernels use flat indices; unrolled and stencil Cauchy paths only apply to Dense.

nodes.label: Sparse Storage Policy
nodes.rationale: Parallel sorted std::vector of idx/val; O(log nnz) lookup; O(nnz_a+nnz_b) add; uses dedicated sparse Cauchy kernel.

nodes.label: Eigen NumTraits Specialization
nodes.rationale: Cost estimates proportional to monomial count C(N+M,M) guide Eigen's cache-aware algorithm selection. RequireInitialization=1 ensures proper construction of TE scalars in Eigen matrices.

nodes.label: Integrator<Stepper,F,Dense>
nodes.rationale: Policy-based design: single Integrator class drives all methods by compile-time Stepper policy. Dense bool decides whether per-step continuous-extension payload is stored.

nodes.label: TaylorStepper<N,State,Controller>
nodes.rationale: DenseData is Eigen::Matrix<TE<N>,D,1> - the per-component time expansion enabling polynomial-Newton root finding and exact N-th-order dense output.

nodes.label: I (Integral) Step Controller
nodes.rationale: Stateless; builds next step purely from current error ratio. Classical baseline.

nodes.label: PI (Gustafsson) Step Controller
nodes.rationale: Default for RK steppers. Adds proportional term over previous error for smoother step sequences.

nodes.label: H211b (Soderlind) Step Controller
nodes.rationale: Second-order digital filter on error history. Not recommended for Taylor - it damps the natural high-order step predictor.

nodes.label: JorbaZou Step Controller
nodes.rationale: Taylor-specific predictor reading magnitudes of last two polynomial coefficients to estimate radius of convergence. Compile-error on non-Taylor steppers by design.

nodes.label: Event<Stepper> (Trigger+Action)
nodes.rationale: Factoring keeps unrelated event types on the same machinery. Trigger decides when; Action decides what. Type-erases callables - captures by reference must outlive integrate().

nodes.label: ZeroCrossing Trigger
nodes.rationale: Taylor path uses polynomial-Newton (3-6 iterations, ULP precision); RK path uses Brent (derivative-free, superlinear). Generic g enables polynomial composition on Taylor path.

nodes.label: Polynomial-Newton Root Finder
nodes.rationale: Composes generic g with per-step TE to get g_poly(tau), then runs safeguarded Newton. Converges 3-6 iterations to ULP. Falls back to bisection-only in pathological cases.

nodes.label: Brent Root Finder
nodes.rationale: Used on RK path where only scalar samples via eval_dense are available. Dekker-Brent with inverse quadratic interpolation and bisection fallback.

nodes.label: Picard Iteration via AD
nodes.rationale: Taylor coefficients computed by propagating DA through RHS. Each x^[k+1] = (1/(k+1)) * f^[k]. Single evaluation of generic RHS on TE-valued state yields entire series.

nodes.label: Dense Output
nodes.rationale: Taylor dense output is exact N-th order (the polynomial is the continuous extension). RK falls back to cubic-Hermite (3rd order), only sufficient for event location and plotting.

nodes.label: RecurrenceStencil / forEachRecurrenceRow
nodes.rationale: Built once at first use (runtime static). Shared decomposition table driving every M>=2 recurrence. With TAX_USE_STENCIL=0 rows are enumerated on the fly, bit-identical results.

nodes.label: Layered Architecture (core/kernels/operators/eigen/ode)
nodes.rationale: Each layer has a single concern: storage manages bytes, kernels manage math, operators manage syntax, Eigen layer manages LA interop, ODE assembles into adaptive solvers.

nodes.label: TruncationCriterion
nodes.rationale: Monitors truncation mass (sum of |c_alpha| for |alpha|=P). Split direction is variable contributing most to that mass. Needs moderately high order to have meaningful tail.

nodes.label: NliCriterion (LOADS)
nodes.rationale: Uses Jacobian-based nonlinearity index (ratio of nonlinear mass to linear part of dPhi/dxi). Works at lower orders than truncation criterion but splits more aggressively.

nodes.label: ADS Refine Driver
nodes.rationale: Propagate whole box to t_final first, then split if quality poor. Embarrassingly parallel since no time-ordering constraints. Costs 3x propagations (self + two trial children) vs classic ADS's 1x.

nodes.label: CoefficientMatchCriterion
nodes.rationale: Compares parent restriction to child coordinates vs independently propagated child. Cheap and dimension-free default.

nodes.label: VolumeRatioCriterion
nodes.rationale: Compares image volume via |det J| integration. V(Phi) should equal V(Phi_L)+V(Phi_R) for accurate parent. More expensive but directly bounds reachable set growth.

nodes.label: Formal Map Inversion (Picard)
nodes.rationale: Picard iteration on non-constant part. Linear part must be invertible (throws std::invalid_argument otherwise). Returns map G such that G∘F = identity up to order N.

hyperedges.label: ODE Stepper, Controller, and Event System
ADS Driver, Split Criterion, and Tree
